objective: Validate Windows native backend candidates for full ccb runtime needs.

candidates.source: temporary portable zip under goal evidence
improvements_over_3_3_3: user_options_title semantic passes in the full gate

gap_specific_observations.attach_reattach: Dedicated attach-survival probe saw session survival, but attach process did not remain running in non-interactive pipe mode; real foreground terminal acceptance remains required.
gap_specific_observations.capture_format_fidelity_for_provider_com: Capture command works, but real evidence still lacks parser fidelity for OSC, wrapping, and wide-character dimensions.
gap_specific_observations.buffer_paste: Manual probe proves load-buffer + paste-buffer + explicit Enter can execute text in a PowerShell pane; provider-pane acceptance is still required.
gap_specific_observations.ctrl_c_ctrl_d: C-c interrupts a long-running Python process; C-d is not Windows EOF. Logical EOF must use a Windows/provider-specific mapping such as C-z Enter where applicable.

critical_observations: start-server and new-session time out when stdout/stderr are captured through pipes
the same lifecycle commands return quickly when stdout/stderr go to DEVNULL or inherited console
reference v8.0.16 implementation already fixed foreground attach by inheriting console stdio
reference RmuxRunner still uses capture_output=True for ordinary commands and does not directly satisfy rmux 0.9.0 daemon-start behavior

overall_verdict: No candidate passes the full ccb Windows native backend gate without product workarounds. psmux 3.3.7 is the preferred base for the next design slice; rmux 0.9.0 remains a research fallback requiring runner and lifecycle changes before it can be reconsidered.
